CORRECTING A PREVIOUSLY-ISSUED LOCAL STORM REPORT FOR LOCATION. AT APPROXIMATELY 330 PM ON JUNE 29TH, FLASH FLOODING WAS OBSERVED AT THE SR12 OVERPASS OF THE ESCALANTE RIVER. ADDITIONAL REPORTS OF FLASH FLOODING WERE OBSERVED UPSTREAM. OTHER REPORTS INCLUDED ESCALANTE RIVER CANYON AND DEATH HOLLOW. THE ESCALANTE RIVER GAUGE CLIMBED FROM NO FLOW TO 400 CFS. AN EARLIER LSR MENTIONED ALVEY WASH, BUT NO FLASH FLOODING WAS OBSERVED THERE.
FLASH FLOODING REPORTED IN ALVEY WASH AND ALONG THE ESCALANTE RIVER.
FIRE DEPARTMENTS REPORTS MULTIPLE ROAD CLOSURES IN THE TOWN, MULTIPLE FLOODED RESIDENCES, AND SEVERAL STRANDED MOTORISTS DUE TO FLASH FLOODING IN THE AREA.
SIGNIFICANT FLASH FLOODING FROM THE ENTRANCE OF ZION NP ACROSS THE EASTERN PORTIONS OF THE PARK. STATE ROUTE 9 WITHIN THE PARK CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING AND DEBRIS ON THE ROADWAY.

REPORT OF WIDESPREAD FLASH FLOODING FROM WIRE PASS TO THE MOUTH OF BUCKSKIN GULCH RECEIVED AROUND 6PM ON 6/29. SEVERAL HIKERS WERE STRANDED BETWEEN WIRE PASS AND THE ENTRANCE TO BUCKSKIN GULCH, WITH MUDDY WATER LEVELS RISING TO WAIST DEEP. ON 6/30 BIG WATER VISITOR CENTER RECEIVED ANOTHER REPORT THAT A GROUP ON A MULTI DAY TRIP WAS TRAPPED WITHIN BUCKSKIN GULCH OVERNIGHT DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. THE GROUP WAS ABLE TO CLIMB ONTO NARROW LEDGES ABOVE THE FLOOD WATERS OVERNIGHT THEN EVAC THE NEXT DAY.

PARK RANGERS REPORT 4 TO 5 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS SCENIC DRIVE AT THE FIRST WASH CROSSING. WATER 6 TO 7 INCHES DEEP WAS REPORTED AT THE HIGHWAY 24 BRIDGE CROSSING THE GRAND WASH. WATERFALLS WERE ALSO OBSERVED IN THE PARK, AND DEBRIS WAS FLOWING DOWN THE FREMONT RIVER.

*** 6 FATAL *** THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WINDS GENERATED BLOWING DUST WITH NEAR ZERO VISIBILITY ALONG INTERSTATE 15 NEAR MP 152 SOUTH OF FILLMORE. THIS RESULTED IN A MULTI VEHICLE PILEUP WITH AT LEAST 22 VEHICLES INVOLVED AND SIX FATALITIES. MULTIPLE INJURIES WERE ALSO REPORTED ALTHOUGH THE EXACT NUMBER OF INJURIES IS UNKNOWN. THE NEAREST RWIS SITE LOCATED 7 MILES SOUTH OF THE CRASH SITE REPORTED A WIND GUST TO 32 MPH AROUND THE TIME OF THE CRASH AROUND 430 PM MDT.

*** 8 FATAL *** UPDATED 26 JULY 12PM. THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WINDS GENERATED BLOWING DUST WITH NEAR ZERO VISIBILITY ALONG INTERSTATE 15 NEAR MP 152 SOUTH OF FILLMORE. THIS RESULTED IN A MULTI VEHICLE PILEUP WITH AT LEAST 22 VEHICLES INVOLVED AND SIX FATALITIES. MULTIPLE INJURIES WERE ALSO REPORTED ALTHOUGH THE EXACT NUMBER OF INJURIES IS UNKNOWN. THE NEAREST RWIS SITE LOCATED 7 MILES SOUTH OF THE CRASH SITE REPORTED A WIND GUST TO 32 MPH AROUND THE TIME OF THE CRASH AROUND 430 PM MDT.

IRON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED SIX FEET OF WATER FLOWED OVER FLOOD CONTROL BARRIER IN PARAGONAH, RESULTING FLASH FLOODING IN THE CITY. SEVERAL HOMES WERE FLOODED. 50 PEOPLE WERE STRANDED IN RED CREEK CANYON EAST OF THE CITY AND WERE SUCCESSFULLY RESCUED. NO KNOWN INJURIES AT THIS TIME. FLOODING STILL ONGOING AT TIME OF REPORT.

NEWS ARTICLE WITH PHOTOS OF SIGNFICIANT TRAIL DAMAGE IN BRYCE CANYON NATIONAL PARK. THE PARK REPROTED WALL STREET SECTION OF NAVAJO LOOP, BRYCE CONNECTOR TRAIL AND PEEKABOO LOOP HAD SIGNFICANT TRAIL DAMAGE FROM HEAVY RAINFALL AND ARE CLOSED. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK REPORTED NOTAM BULLFROG ROAD WAS WASHED OUT AND IMPASSIBLE DUE TO FLASH FLOODING SOUTH OF SANDY RANCH AT DOG WATER WASH. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK REPORTED BURR TRAIL, STRIKE VALLEY AND HALLS CREEK ROAD WERE ALL IMPASSIBLE. THIS WAS DISCOVERED THE FOLLOWING DAY ALONG WITH LOWER MULEY TWIST STILL HAD 3 TO 4 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER AT THAT TIME. TIME OF FLASH FLOODING ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
IRON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED AN OLD DIKE COLLAPSED DUE TO HEAVY RAINFALL WITH A LARGE AMOUNT OF DEBRIS ACROSS OLD US 91 CLOSING THE ROAD.

ZION NATIONAL PARK REPORTED KOLOB CANYONS SECTION OF THE PARK WAS CLOSED DUE TO A LARGE AMOUNT OF MUD AND ROCK SLIDES FROM OVERNIGHT FLASH FLOODING. EXACT TIME OF FLOODING UNKNOW DUE TO OVERNIGHT TIMING, BUT ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
RIVER GAGE ON THE EAST FORK OF THE VIRGIN RIVER NEAR GLENDALE ROSE TO 544 CFS, WHICH CORRESPONDS TO A 3 FOOT RISE.
THE RIVER GAGE ON THE ESCALANTE RIVER NEAR ESCALANTE ROSE TO 1440 CFS, WHICH CORRESPONDS TO A RISE OF OVER 5 FEET.

CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK ESTIMATED PLEASANT CREEK AND SHEETS GULCH HAD 6-8 FEET OF FLOODING OVERNIGHT, AND BURRO, COTTONWOOD, AND 5 MILE WASHES HAD 2 TO 4 FEET OF FLOODING. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK REPORTED SCENIC DRIVE WAS CLOSED AT GRAND WASH DUE TO A LARGE AMOUNT OF MUD ACROSS THE ROAD MAKING IT IMPASSIBLE. EXACT TIME OF THE FLASH FLOOD NOT KNOW DUE TO EARLY MORNING HOURS, BUT ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

ZION NP DISPATCH REPORTED A ROCKSLIDE DUE TO EXCESSIVE RAINFALL ALONG THE SCENIC DRIVE NORTH OF THE VISITOR CENTER. A PORTION OF THE ROAD WAS DAMAGED DUE TO THE SLIDE AND IS CLOSED AS A RESULT. LOCATION APPROXIMATE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
THE RIVER GAGE ON THE NORTH FORK OF THE VIRGIN RIVER NEAR SPRINGDALE HAD A SHARP RISE AND PEAKED AT 1690 CFS. THIS CORRESPONDS TO A RISE OF OVER 3 FEET.
THE RIVER GAGE AT THE EAST FORK OF THE VIRGIN RIVER NEAR SPRINGDALE ROSE TO 1970 CFS, WHICH CORRESPONDS TO A 5 FOOT RISE.

CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK REPORTED NOTAM BULLFROG ROAD WAS ALSO WASHED OUT AT BULLFROG CREEK CROSSING NEAR GLEN CANYON NATIONAL RECREATION AREA. THERE WAS A 5 FOOT DROP OFF AND THE ROAD IS IMPASSIBLE. EXACT TIME UNCLEAR, BUT ESTIMATED FROM REPORT TIME AND RADAR ESTIMATED PRECIPITATION.
CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK REPORTED FLASH FLOODING IN GRAND WASH WITH 6 TO 8 INCHES OF RAPIDLY FLOWING WATER.
MANTI PORTABLE RAWS ON A RIDGETOP AT 9877 FEET RECORDED A PEAK WIND GUST OF 75 MPH FROM A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
